data warehouse consolidation.text: The Single Data Source Myth: Why Moving All Enterprise Data Into One Place May Not Work for…  Medium

summary: The article discusses the limitations of moving all enterprise data into one place, citing examples of companies that have tried and failed.
It argues that a single data source may not work for every organization due to various reasons such as data volume, complexity, and governance requirements.
The article suggests that a more effective approach might be to adopt a data fabric or a federated query engine to integrate data from multiple sources.
analysis.pain_point: Data silos and the limitations of moving all enterprise data into one place
analysis.zetaris_relevance: Low relevance — The article discusses a general problem with a data warehouse or data lake approach, but does not mention Zetaris's capabilities or a specific customer.
